config.title: Vietnam, three rhythms
config.destination: Hanoi · Hoi An · Ho Chi Minh City
config.coverNote: I built this as a food-first trip through Vietnam, with Hanoi's disciplined northern flavors, Hoi An's market and trading-port cooking, and Saigon's street-level abundance giving the route its shape. The hotels protect value and recovery time, while the meals, guides, and markets carry the story. Nothing here is represented as booked. The 2027 dates and rates are a transparent planning proxy for the 2028 trip you requested, and I will reprice and reconfirm every component before anything is reserved.
config.driverPhrase: Làm ơn đưa tôi đến đây.

days.title: Fly to Vietnam
days.summary: Depart IAD at 11:50 AM on the live planning itinerary.

days.title: Arrive in Hanoi
days.summary: Private transfer, check in, and keep the first evening deliberately light.

days.title: Old Quarter and food
days.summary: A guided walk through Hanoi's historic core with a serious street-food introduction.

days.title: Ideas and memory
days.summary: Temple of Literature, Hoa Lo, and the Vietnamese Women's Museum.

days.title: Art and living culture
days.summary: Fine Arts Museum, a focused architecture or craft walk, and an open evening.

days.title: Hanoi to Hoi An
days.summary: Fly to Da Nang, transfer to Hoi An, and take a first slow loop through Ancient Town.

days.title: My Son before the buses
days.summary: Early private guide to the Cham sanctuary, with the afternoon protected.

days.title: Market to table
days.summary: Morning market and cooking experience, then pool, tailor, spa, or café time.

days.title: Choose your pace
days.summary: Countryside and Tra Que, or a completely unscheduled day.

days.title: Hoi An to Saigon
days.summary: Fly to Ho Chi Minh City, settle in, then a rooftop drink and easy dinner.

days.title: The twentieth century
days.summary: Reunification Palace and War Remnants Museum with contextual guidance.

days.title: Chợ Lớn and street food
days.summary: Chinatown temples and markets, then an evening tasting route.

days.title: Art, design, and dinner
days.summary: Fine Arts Museum, independent galleries, and a chef-led dinner.

days.title: One open day
days.summary: Optional Cu Chi, optional Mekong, or stay in the city for a slow final day.

days.title: Begin the trip home
days.summary: Late checkout if available, fly Saigon to Hanoi, then connect to IAD.

days.title: Arrive home
days.summary: Land at IAD at 9:50 AM on the live planning itinerary.

itinerary.title: IAD to Hanoi via Seoul
itinerary.subtitle: Korean Air / Delta planning itinerary · $1,306 per adult round trip
itinerary.personalNote: I chose IAD because it keeps the long-haul on a practical one-ticket itinerary.

itinerary.title: Hanoi Tirant Hotel
itinerary.subtitle: $102 per night live display · 4 nights
itinerary.personalNote: My balanced Hanoi pick: central, polished, and good value without paying for resort facilities in a city stay.

itinerary.title: Private Old Quarter food walk
itinerary.personalNote: This is one of the two experiences I would lock early. A strong guide matters more than collecting a long restaurant list.

itinerary.title: Tầm Vị dinner
itinerary.subtitle: Priority reservation · polished northern Vietnamese cooking
itinerary.personalNote: The composed dinner after a history-heavy day. I would reserve this once 2028 inventory opens.

itinerary.title: Chả Cá Thăng Long
itinerary.subtitle: Focused final Hanoi dinner
itinerary.personalNote: A single-dish specialist is the right final note for Hanoi.

itinerary.title: Hanoi to Da Nang
itinerary.subtitle: Nonstop domestic flight · exact schedule to be selected

details.Planning allowance: Included in $360 to $520 domestic-flight range for two with checked bags

itinerary.title: La Siesta Hoi An Resort & Spa
itinerary.subtitle: $111 per night live display · 4 nights
itinerary.personalNote: This is where I would take the resort upgrade. It changes the middle of the trip without distorting the budget.
itinerary.address: 132 Hùng Vương, Thanh Hà, Hoi An

itinerary.title: Morning Glory Original
itinerary.subtitle: Priority reservation · first-night central Vietnamese survey
itinerary.personalNote: Easy enough after the transfer, specific enough to begin Hoi An properly.

itinerary.title: Private early visit to My Son Sanctuary
itinerary.personalNote: Go early, before the larger tour groups and the strongest heat.

itinerary.title: Cao Lầu Thanh lunch
itinerary.subtitle: Family-run specialty stop after My Son
itinerary.personalNote: Go early enough that the day's noodles are still available.

itinerary.title: Hoi An market and cooking experience
itinerary.personalNote: I would choose a small class that treats the market as part of the lesson, not a photo stop.

itinerary.title: Da Nang to Ho Chi Minh City
itinerary.subtitle: Nonstop domestic flight · exact schedule to be selected

itinerary.title: Mai House Saigon Hotel
itinerary.subtitle: $144 per night live display · 5 nights
itinerary.personalNote: A proper five-star finish without trophy-hotel pricing.
itinerary.address: 1-3-5 Ngô Thời Nhiệm, District 3, Ho Chi Minh City

itinerary.title: Bánh Xèo 46A
itinerary.subtitle: Casual first Saigon dinner
itinerary.personalNote: A crisp, hands-on dinner that does not ask too much after the flight.

itinerary.title: Private Saigon evening tasting route
itinerary.subtitle: Four food stops plus one drink · driver-guided
itinerary.personalNote: The guide should build the route around ốc, bò lá lốt, and a dish not already covered in Hanoi or Hoi An.

itinerary.title: Ho Chi Minh City Museum of Fine Arts
itinerary.locationName: Ho Chi Minh City Museum of Fine Arts

itinerary.title: Ănăn Saigon
itinerary.subtitle: Priority reservation · trip-closing dinner
itinerary.personalNote: The serious table at the end of the trip, after Jesse has enough context to appreciate what the kitchen is changing.

itinerary.title: Phở Minh breakfast
itinerary.subtitle: Southern phở comparison
itinerary.personalNote: Finish the north-versus-south noodle comparison, then leave the rest of the day open.

itinerary.title: Ho Chi Minh City to Hanoi
itinerary.subtitle: Same-day positioning flight for the international connection
itinerary.personalNote: I would protect a long same-airport buffer in Hanoi and book this on a dependable schedule.

picks.name: Phở Gia Truyền Bát Đàn
picks.description: A classic northern phở specialist known for a clear, beef-forward broth.
picks.note: Go early for breakfast, accept the queue, and order rare beef or brisket. This is the baseline bowl for comparing northern and southern phở.

picks.name: Bún Chả Hương Liên
picks.address: 24 Lê Văn Hưu, Hai Bà Trưng, Hanoi
picks.description: Charcoal pork patties and slices served with rice noodles, herbs, and dipping broth.
picks.note: A 2026 MICHELIN selection and a useful guided lunch stop. Expect fame and crowds, but the dish still explains Hanoi clearly.

picks.name: Chả Cá Thăng Long
picks.description: Turmeric-marinated fish cooked at the table with dill and scallions.
picks.note: Make this a focused dinner. Order the house specialty, use the herbs generously, and skip extra dishes unless still hungry.

picks.name: Café Giảng
picks.address: 39 Nguyễn Hữu Huân, Hoàn Kiếm, Hanoi
picks.description: The historic home of Hanoi's custard-like egg coffee.
picks.note: Use this as a short afternoon reset, not a full café meal. The original Old Quarter address is the one I would choose.

picks.name: Tầm Vị
picks.description: A polished northern Vietnamese table in a calm, traditional setting.
picks.note: One of the three reservations I would prioritize. It gives Jesse a composed counterpoint to the street-level meals.

picks.name: Chè 95
picks.description: A quick Old Quarter stop for chè and other Vietnamese sweet soups.
picks.note: Treat this as dessert during the food walk. Ask the guide which bowl is strongest that day.

picks.name: Cao Lầu Thanh
picks.description: A long-running family specialist for Hoi An's chewy cao lầu noodles.
picks.note: Go for an early lunch before the day's batch is gone. Order cao lầu and resist turning it into a long meal.

picks.name: White Rose Restaurant
picks.description: The family restaurant associated with Hoi An's translucent white rose dumplings.
picks.note: Order the dumplings as a focused snack or share them before another meal. This is about a local specialty, not atmosphere.

picks.name: Cơm Gà Bà Buội
picks.description: A decades-old family specialist for Hoi An chicken rice with herbs and bright fish sauce.
picks.note: Best as a light lunch. If the queue is severe, the guide can substitute a current local chicken-rice specialist rather than forcing it.

picks.name: Mì Quảng Dì Hát
picks.description: A local bowl of mì Quảng with turmeric noodles, herbs, broth, and rice cracker.
picks.note: Use this as the central-Vietnam noodle comparison. Go earlier in the day and let the available protein guide the order.
picks.hours: Morning or early lunch; reconfirm before travel

picks.name: Morning Glory Original
picks.description: A polished survey of central Vietnamese home and street dishes.
picks.note: The right first-night dinner after the flight and transfer. Reserve it, order regionally, and keep the evening easy.

picks.name: Madam Khánh, the Bánh Mì Queen
picks.description: A fast-moving bánh mì counter with a layered house combination.
picks.note: Treat it as a snack or light lunch between walks. It should not displace the cooking morning or the noodle specialists.

picks.name: Cơm Tấm Ba Ghiền
picks.address: 84 Đặng Văn Ngữ, Phú Nhuận, Ho Chi Minh City
picks.description: A 2026 MICHELIN Bib Gourmand for broken rice and an exceptionally large grilled pork chop.
picks.note: Go for an early lunch and share if the chop is as large as expected. This is a destination meal outside the central districts.

picks.name: Bánh Xèo 46A
picks.address: 46A Đinh Công Tráng, District 1, Ho Chi Minh City
picks.description: A 2026 MICHELIN Bib Gourmand for crisp turmeric pancakes with pork, shrimp, and herbs.
picks.note: A relaxed first-night dinner. Let the staff show the wrapping technique and do not over-order before the pancakes arrive.

picks.name: Phở Minh
picks.address: 63/3 Pasteur, District 1, Ho Chi Minh City
picks.description: A traditional Saigon phở shop serving beef noodle soup since 1945.
picks.note: Breakfast here completes the north-versus-south phở comparison. Go early and order the cut of beef you preferred in Hanoi.

picks.name: Ốc Đào
picks.address: Hẻm 212B Nguyễn Trãi, District 1, Ho Chi Minh City
picks.description: A neighborhood seafood specialist for snails, clams, crab, garlic, and tamarind sauces.
picks.note: Best with the private tasting guide, who can control the order and explain sauces, shells, and pacing.

picks.name: Cô Liêng
picks.address: 321 Võ Văn Tần, District 3, Ho Chi Minh City
picks.description: A casual specialist for bò lá lốt rolled with herbs and rice paper.
picks.note: Use it as one stop on the tasting route, not a standalone formal dinner. The guide should confirm the strongest branch and current service.

picks.name: Ănăn Saigon
picks.address: 89 Tôn Thất Đạm, District 1, Ho Chi Minh City
picks.description: MICHELIN-starred contemporary Vietnamese cooking above a traditional market district.
picks.note: The trip-closing reservation. Choose tasting or à la carte based on the final menu, and protect the evening from other plans.

picks.name: Temple of Literature
picks.description: Vietnam's first national university and one of Hanoi's clearest windows into Confucian scholarly culture.
picks.note: Go with a guide or read before you arrive. The symbolism rewards context.

picks.name: Vietnamese Women's Museum
picks.description: An unusually strong social-history museum centered on women's lives, family, work, and conflict.
picks.note: I would not skip this. It adds human texture that the larger state sites cannot.

picks.name: Hoi An Ancient Town
picks.description: A preserved trading-port core of merchant houses, assembly halls, temples, and river streets.
picks.note: Walk it once early and once after dark. The mood changes completely.

picks.name: Reunification Palace
picks.description: A preserved modernist government complex tied directly to the end of the Vietnam War.
picks.note: The rooms make more sense with a guide who can explain the building as well as the politics.

picks.name: War Remnants Museum
picks.description: A powerful and intentionally Vietnamese presentation of wartime experience.
picks.note: Give it time, then leave space afterward. This is not a quick photo stop.

picks.name: Ho Chi Minh City Museum of Fine Arts
picks.description: Vietnamese art in a striking colonial-era building near the center.
picks.note: Pair it with contemporary gallery stops so the art story does not end in the museum.

picks.name: The Workshop Coffee
picks.description: A specialty-coffee room above the city center's street noise.
picks.note: Use it as a deliberate mid-afternoon reset.

picks.name: Hoa Lo Prison Relic
picks.description: A layered site covering French colonial imprisonment and the later American-war story.
picks.note: Use a guide or audio context, and pair it with the Women's Museum rather than another heavy site.

picks.name: Vietnam National Fine Arts Museum
picks.address: 66 Nguyễn Thái Học, Ba Đình, Hanoi
picks.description: Vietnamese art from antiquity through modern periods in a former colonial school.
picks.note: Focus on lacquer, folk traditions, and twentieth-century work. Two deliberate hours are enough.

picks.name: My Son Sanctuary
picks.description: A UNESCO-listed Cham temple complex in a green valley west of Hoi An.
picks.note: Go early with a private guide. The history matters more than adding another stop on the return drive.

picks.name: Trà Quế Vegetable Village
picks.description: A working garden village supplying herbs and vegetables to Hoi An kitchens.
picks.note: Keep it optional. It works only if Jesse wants a quiet countryside morning after the cooking class.

contacts.name: Gabe
contacts.note: Text me first. If the plan needs to move, I will help move it.

guideBlocks.heading: What is and is not booked
guideBlocks.body: This is a researched planning itinerary. Every flight, room, guide, transfer, ticket, restaurant, price, and cancellation policy remains pending until you approve it and I send a booking confirmation.

guideBlocks.heading: Why IAD, not DCA
guideBlocks.body: IAD offers the practical one-ticket transpacific options. Starting at DCA would add a domestic positioning leg and more failure points without improving the long-haul fare enough to justify it.

guideBlocks.heading: 2028 pricing method
guideBlocks.body: 2028 airfare is not yet in the normal public sales window. I priced exact February 20 to March 6, 2027 inventory as a same-season proxy. The hub shows the planning floor, not a bookable 2028 quote.

guideBlocks.heading: Getting around
guideBlocks.body: Use private airport transfers on arrival days. In Hanoi and Saigon, Grab is the simplest everyday option. In Hoi An, walk the Ancient Town and use a car for My Son, the countryside, and Da Nang airport.

guideBlocks.heading: Power and outlets
guideBlocks.body: Vietnam uses 220V. Outlets commonly accept Type A, C, and sometimes F plugs. Phones and laptops are usually dual voltage, but check hair tools. Pack one compact universal adapter per traveler and keep a charging brick in your carry-on.

guideBlocks.heading: Food pacing
guideBlocks.body: I would prebook the Hanoi food guide, the Hoi An market-to-table cooking morning, the Saigon tasting route, and three dinners: Tầm Vị, Morning Glory Original, and Ănăn Saigon. The specialist noodle, rice, dessert, and seafood stops stay flexible and are reconfirmed close to travel.

guideBlocks.heading: How to use the food map
guideBlocks.body: The map holds 18 researched food anchors, but it is not a checklist. Each city has one guided experience, one or two priority reservations, and flexible specialists for breakfast, lunch, snacks, and market evenings. If hours, hygiene, or quality change before 2028, I will replace the stop rather than force the old name.

guideBlocks.heading: Food budget
guideBlocks.body: Plan $1,100 to $1,500 for two across the trip. That supports street food, regional specialists, the three reservation dinners, coffee and drinks, while leaving room to change the mix once 2028 menus and prices are available.

essentials.label: U.S. Embassy Hanoi
essentials.note: Reconfirm emergency contact procedures before travel.

essentials.label: U.S. Consulate Ho Chi Minh City
essentials.address: 4 Le Duan Boulevard, District 1, Ho Chi Minh City
essentials.note: Reconfirm emergency contact procedures before travel.

essentials.label: Emergency
essentials.note: Use the dedicated numbers above. Ask the hotel or guide to help communicate the location in Vietnamese.

essentials.label: Hospital and international clinic · Hanoi
essentials.address: 298 I Kim Mã Road, Vạn Phúc Compound, Ba Đình, Hanoi
essentials.note: 24/7 international-standard clinic. Reconfirm the location and number before travel.

essentials.label: Hospital · Hoi An
essentials.note: Private multi-specialty hospital with an international-patient service. Reconfirm before travel.

essentials.label: Hospital and international clinic · Saigon
essentials.address: 34 Lê Duẩn, District 1, Ho Chi Minh City
essentials.note: Central international clinic. Reconfirm 24/7 coverage and contact details before travel.

essentials.label: Pharmacy
essentials.note: A nationwide pharmacy chain. Ask the hotel to identify the nearest open branch and bring prescriptions in original packaging.
